name: Joseph Cabrera
background: Business owner from the Inland Empire. Self-described independent and non-career politician.
statement: California was built on a promise: that through hard work and innovation, any citizen could build a prosperous life. Today, that promise is fading under partisan gridlock and a $31 billion transportation budget that leaves our roads crumbling. I am running for Governor to provide the 'Independent Fix' our state desperately needs. Sacramento politicians are asking for new taxes, but their own budget—Schedule 11—shows they are sitting on $30.1 billion in unspent, voter-approved bond money. We have nearly $500 million for traffic reduction sitting idle since 2006. As a business owner, I know you don't ask for a new credit card when you haven't used the one in your pocket. We must also confront our humanitarian crises. Homelessness, plummeting school performance, and our mental health crisis are all symptoms of the same failed bureaucracy. For too long, we have used our jails as warehouses for the mentally ill while allowing our streets to become encampments. I will shift resources away from bloat and into community treatment, focusing on recovery instead of a revolving prison door. Finally, we must restore and revamp the Donahoe Higher Education Act. I will return our public universities to the merit-based, accessible model that once made California the envy of the world. By auditing 'ghost projects' and redirecting interest savings, we can fund our schools and clinics without raising your taxes.

positions.economy: No new taxes; use existing unspent voter-approved bond money ($30.1B); audit ghost projects
positions.healthcare: Mental health community treatment; shift resources from jails to recovery
positions.education: Restore merit-based public universities; revamp Donahoe Higher Education Act; fund schools without new taxes
positions.publicSafety: Homelessness reform; mental health treatment over incarceration
positions.government: End partisan gridlock; independent leadership; use existing budget before asking for new taxes

contact.address: 1941 California Ave., PO Box 77217, Corona, CA 92887
